input.prompt:
Determine whether Cursor supports these slash commands and how the user should install the referenced plugin:
/plugin marketplace add nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
/plugin install ui-ux-pro-max@ui-ux-pro-max-skill
Use current Cursor product documentation. Return a concise, concrete answer and any supported alternative in Cursor. Do not modify files.

input.command: command -v cursor-agent || true; command -v cursor || true
npm view ui-ux-pro-max-cli version dist-tags repository --json
input.description: Verify UI UX installer package metadata
Install UI UX skill for Cursor

content.text:
**Verifying files**

I need to verify if any files have changed, specifically focusing on the ".cursor/skills" directory. It's important for me to make sure that everything is up to date and functioning as expected. I'll go through the relevant files in that directory to check for any modifications. This way, I can ensure nothing is out of place and confirm that the files are in the right condition for use.

**Identifying installed packages**

I’m noticing that a lot more was installed than just ui-ux. It seems like package v2.11 brought in quite a few skills along with it. I really need to identify which skills are relevant to what I’m working on right now. Maybe there’s something specific like ui-ux-pro-max that I should focus on. Let's make sure I'm on the right track with this!

Installed UI/UX Pro Max project-locally at `.cursor/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/`.

Restart Cursor to activate it. The installer also added six related design skills under `.cursor/skills/`.

<timestamp>Sunday, Jul 19, 2026, 5:38 PM (UTC+4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
npx skills add nutlope/hallmark
install this also
</user_query>

input.description: Install hallmark skill via skills CLI

content.text: The skills CLI installed to `.agents/skills/hallmark` and set up symlinks for multiple agents including Cursor, so now I need to verify that Cursor can actually discover it since it looks for skills under `.cursor/skills/`.

input.command: ls -la .agents/skills/hallmark 2>/dev/null; echo "---cursor skills---"; ls -la .cursor/skills 2>/dev/null | grep -i hall
input.description: Verify hallmark install location and Cursor linkage

content.text: The skill got installed to `.agents/skills/hallmark` but wasn't linked into `.cursor/skills`, even though the summary mentioned copying it to Cursor and other tools. I'm checking whether Cursor has a symlink or configuration pointing to `.agents/skills`, since modern Cursor might support that shared agent directory now. Let me look for an AGENTS.md file or symlink that could explain the setup.

input.command: find .cursor .agents -maxdepth 3 -name '*hallmark*' 2>/dev/null; echo "---symlinks in .cursor/skills---"; ls -la .cursor/skills; echo "---.agents tree---"; ls -la .agents
input.description: Locate hallmark references and any symlinks

content.text:
The skills CLI put hallmark under `.agents/skills/` but didn't link it into `.cursor/skills/` where Cursor discovers project skills (unlike the ui-ux-pro-max install). Let me link it so Cursor picks it up.

The skills CLI installed hallmark to `.agents/skills/hallmark` instead of `.cursor/skills/`, which is where Cursor actually looks for skills. While Cursor does recognize `AGENTS.md`, its skill discovery mechanism specifically targets `.cursor/skills/` directories at the project or home level, not the `.agents/` path that the skills.sh convention uses. I'm wondering if newer versions of Cursor might have changed this behavior.

To make sure hallmark is discoverable by Cursor the same way ui-ux-pro-max is, I'll create a symlink from `.agents/skills/hallmark` into `.cursor/skills/hallmark`.

input.description: Symlink hallmark into Cursor skills dir

content.text:
Installed. `hallmark` is now available in Cursor.

- The `skills` CLI installed it to `.agents/skills/hallmark/` (its canonical location).
- It didn't auto-link into `.cursor/skills/`, so I symlinked `.cursor/skills/hallmark` → `.agents/skills/hallmark` so Cursor discovers it alongside `ui-ux-pro-max`.

Restart/reload Cursor to pick it up. Security scan reported it clean (Socket: 0 alerts, Snyk: low risk).

<timestamp>Sunday, Jul 19, 2026, 5:41 PM (UTC+4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
how to use this?
</user_query>
I'll pull the usage section from the installed Hallmark skill so the answer matches what's actually in the repo.

Just talk to me in normal language — Hallmark kicks in when you ask for design/UI work, or when you use one of its verbs.

### Default — build something new
Ask for a page or UI and I follow Hallmark’s design flow (structure, theme, anti-slop rules):

- “Build a landing page for …”
- “Design the settings screen for …”
- “Hallmark: make a pricing page for …”

### Explicit verbs

| Say this | What happens |
|---|---|
| `hallmark audit <file or page>` | Scores the UI against anti-patterns; ranked punch list only — **no edits** |
| `hallmark redesign <target>` | Redesigns look/structure **in place**; keeps routes, brand, IA unless you approve a rebuild |
| `hallmark study <screenshot or URL>` | Extracts design DNA (layout, type, color); then you can build from it or lock a `design.md` |

### Examples you can paste

```text
hallmark audit frontend/src/pages/Board.tsx
```

```text
hallmark redesign the marketing homepage --mood editorial
```

```text
hallmark study https://example.com
```

```text
Build a landing page for a SaaS that helps engineers find relocation jobs
```

### Notes for this repo
- Hallmark is loaded as a skill; no extra install step when chatting.
- On an existing app it prefers **in-place** edits and will ask before deleting files.
- If you drop a screenshot/URL without a verb, I’ll ask: study DNA, or use as reference for a fresh build?

If you want a concrete first run, say which target (a page path, a URL, or a brief) and which verb.
